action: Open Notepad on the computer by clicking the Start menu, searching for Notepad, and launching the application.
fallback_action: Perform a Google search using Internet Explorer

recipe.steps:
is the first action. The task is to open Notepad by clicking the Start menu, searching for Notepad, and launching it. On the current desktop screen, the Windows Start button is typically located at the bottom left, but in the detected bounding boxes, Box ID 75 is labeled 'Windows' and visually resembles the Start button. I will click this to open the Start menu.
Action Description: Click the Windows Start button to open the Start menu.

previous action: The previous step was to click the Windows Start button (Box ID 75) to open the Start menu. On the current screen, the Start menu is now visible in the lower left, showing a search bar and various app icons. This confirms the previous action succeeded. The next step is to search for Notepad by typing 'Notepad' into the Start menu's search bar.
Action Description: Type 'Notepad' into the Start menu search bar to search for the Notepad application.

previous action: The previous step was to type 'Notepad' into the Start menu search bar. On the current screen, the Start menu is still open and the search results are visible. I can see the Notepad application icon and label among the results, indicating that the search was successful. The next step is to launch Notepad by clicking on its icon in the search results.
Action Description: Click the Notepad application icon in the Start menu search results to launch Notepad.
